what_it_does: Records the grounding-batch result for the C1-inbox-desktop.html mockup (the Chief-of-Staff Inbox triage dashboard): a 'ground' summary, counts (43/43/28), and 14 confirmed vs 15 flagged UI-element dossiers, each with a ui:// address, capabilities, grounding state, plain-language howto, feature mapping, and accuracy-jury (plus occasional refcheck) verdict.
observations: Screen is a three-lane triage Inbox: live escalations awaiting decision, resolved-for-you (audit), and a batched lane.
Dossiers cover wire/force/run actions, decision cards, build-review cards, CoA (course-of-action) controls, idea/propose cards, lane headers, counters and footers.
Decision-card dossier encodes a principle: the operator decides on value (Commander-altitude: 2-3 options + trade-offs + recommendation), never the raw code fork; compiler is suite.py:coa.
An 'excluded' counter element shows items filtered by a test_origin tag, visible only as a count (cites suite.py:965-967).
build-review cards encode 'demonstrate-first': read the instruction / see it working before accept or send-back-with-a-why.
Three Layer-1 refcheck feature-fabrication failures: INB-focus, INB-excluded, INB-propose (not inventory ids nor 'proposed').
Two flagged items (decision-focus, excluded) have jury verdict=true yet are FLAGGED solely due to the deterministic refcheck floor.
The address ui://inbox/idea appears in three distinct dossiers (one confirmed, two flagged) with different represents/grounding.
howto.how_to_change names Inbox.tsx, suite.py:inbox_lanes, suite.py:coa, suite.py:review_verdicts, Toolbar.tsx.
standouts: Encodes the 'decide on value not on code' principle as a first-class decision card (INB-decision, 4-member cluster, unanimously confirmed).
'Demonstrate-first' review discipline appears as an addressable remark.
Refcheck floor here flips two would-be-confirmed (jury-true) items to flagged on a hard feature-id check.
conventions: Same confirmed/flagged + two-layer grading as sibling rg10 files.
ui:// addressing across inbox, inbox/coa, inbox/build-review namespaces.
Plain-language howto triad with a concrete code location per dossier.
Some maps_to_feature values are 'proposed' (permitted) while several fabricated ids are caught.
concerns: Address reuse: ui://inbox/idea maps to three different dossiers in one file.
Multiple feature-fabrication failures (INB-focus, INB-excluded, INB-propose) suggest the dossiers invent feature ids outside the inventory.
jury-true-but-flagged cases show the two grading layers can disagree, leaving verdict interpretation non-obvious from the file alone.
notes: The inbox triage dashboard inventory — 14 confirmed, 15 flagged — notable for three fabricated feature-ids caught by the deterministic refcheck and the 'decide on value not code' principle.
questions: When jury verdict is true but a refcheck fails, what is the final disposition for that element?
Should the three distinct ui://inbox/idea dossiers be merged or are they genuinely different elements?
What is the authoritative feature-inventory that INB-focus / INB-excluded / INB-propose failed against?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: declared as a grounding result for the inbox desktop mockup and that is its content.
apparent_themes: Decision triage at Commander altitude (value, not code)
Two-layer grounding: a hard feature-id floor plus a soft accuracy jury
intention_signals: It appears to be verifying which inbox-triage controls are grounded in built code while enforcing that each element maps to a real feature id, catching fabricated mappings and flagging uncertain groundings for the operator.
novelty: Shows the deterministic refcheck floor actively overriding a positive jury vote on a feature-id fabrication, demonstrating the two grading layers in tension.
contribution: Contributes the Chief-of-Staff inbox element inventory — three-lane triage, value-altitude decision cards, build-review and CoA controls, exclusion counters — with addresses, howtos, and two-layer grounding verdicts including feature-fabrication catches.
summary_for_embedding: Grounding-batch JSON for the inbox-triage desktop mockup, listing 43 clustered UI dossiers (decision cards, build-review, CoA, lanes) with 14 confirmed and 15 flagged via refcheck-plus-jury grading and three caught fabricated feature ids.
intention_for_embedding: To check whether inbox-triage controls are grounded in built code and map to real features, flagging uncertain or fabricated mappings for operator review.
